message:
feat(runtime_compat): tick 161 JoinSet::join_next_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Twelfth post-storage tick. Closes one of the two deferred
runtime_compat targets from tick 160.

New sibling:
- JoinSet::join_next_with_cx — pre-flight cx.checkpoint gates
  entry, AND each subsequent poll-loop iteration also invokes
  cx.checkpoint before scanning handles. This is the key
  difference from a pre-flight-only wrapper: a cancellation that
  arrives mid-wait surfaces within the same tick as the cancel
  signal rather than waiting for the next task completion.

Implementation note: the poll_fn closure conventionally names its
`std::task::Context` as `cx`, so the outer capability context is
bound to `caller_cx` (parameter name) to avoid name collision
inside the poll body.

Error path: cancellation surfaces as
`Some(Err(JoinError { msg: "aborted: join_next cancelled: ..." }))`.
The "aborted" prefix triggers `JoinError::is_cancelled() == true`
(existing convention at L1017: `JoinError::new("task aborted")`),
letting downstream match arms fold cancel into their normal
task-failure path without branching on a new error variant.

Test: `join_next_with_cx_short_circuits_on_precancelled_cx` pins
the contract with a never-completing `std::future::pending` task
— a pre-cancelled cx must produce `Some(Err(is_cancelled))`
immediately. If the checkpoint was moved after the handle scan
or omitted, this test would hang (pending future) rather than
failing — so the runtime itself enforces the contract.

Verified via fork bypass: 193/193 runtime_compat:: tests pass
(+1 from 192).

Remaining runtime_compat target: `process::Command::output`
(spawn_blocking body with kill_on_drop semantics, still complex).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
